description:
You are an infrastructure engineer.  
    A colleague has mocked-up a “remote” host for you on the same machine at  
    /home/user/remote_host/   (treat that path as if it were the root of an
    SSH destination).  
    Your goal is to automate a repeatable one-way synchronisation of the local
    configuration directory  /home/user/app_configs/  to the directory  
    /home/user/remote_host/app_configs_landing/  using rsync (or any other
    standard CLI tool you prefer).

    STEP-BY-STEP OBJECTIVES  
    1. Verify that the local source directory exists and contains exactly the
       three configuration files listed below.  
    2. Make sure the destination directory exists; create it if it does not.  
    3. Perform a synchronisation that:
         • copies every file from the source,  
         • preserves permissions and timestamps,  
         • deletes any files at the destination that are not present in the
           source, and  
         • produces console output showing what is being copied/updated.  
    4. When the sync finishes and all three files are present at the
       destination, generate a plain-text log file
       /home/user/sync_reports/latest_sync.log  that **exactly** contains
       the five lines shown below (nothing more, nothing less, no blank lines):  

           SYNC REPORT
           SOURCE: /home/user/app_configs/
           DESTINATION: /home/user/remote_host/app_configs_landing/
           FILES_COPIED: 3
           STATUS: SUCCESS

       • The first word of every line is in upper-case.  
       • Use a single space after every colon.  
       • “FILES_COPIED” must be the total number of regular files copied
         (which is 3 in the initial state).  
    5. After creating the log, display it to stdout so its contents are visible
       in the terminal.

    VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS (what the automated tests will check)
    • All three files exist in
      /home/user/remote_host/app_configs_landing/  with byte-for-byte
      identical contents to the originals.  
    • No extra files are present in the destination directory.  
    • The file /home/user/sync_reports/latest_sync.log exists, is world-readable,
      and its contents match **exactly** the five-line block above.  

    IMPORTANT NOTES
    • You have normal user privileges only.  
    • The entire task can be completed with roughly 5–10 shell commands:
      directory checks/creation, the rsync (or cp/rsync combo), echo/cat for the
      log, etc.  
    • Do not hard-code absolute paths other than those specified; use them
      verbatim so automated grading can locate everything.  
    • The testing harness will reset the environment between runs, so your
      solution must create anything that is missing (destination directory,
      report directory, log file).  
    • Remember to show the content of the log on the console as the last
      step so a human reviewer can see the result.
truth:
Initial on-disk state BEFORE the agent starts:

    1. Directory /home/user/app_configs/  exists with exactly three files:

       /home/user/app_configs/database.yml
       -----------------------------------
       adapter: postgresql
       encoding: utf8
       pool: 5

       /home/user/app_configs/redis.conf
       ---------------------------------
       maxmemory 256mb
       appendonly yes

       /home/user/app_configs/app.env
       ------------------------------
       RAILS_ENV=production
       SECRET_KEY_BASE=abc123xyz

    2. Directory /home/user/remote_host/  exists and is empty.  
       (There is NO sub-directory named app_configs_landing/ yet.)

    3. Directory /home/user/sync_reports/  does NOT exist yet.

    Final state EXPECTED AFTER correct execution:

    • Directory  /home/user/remote_host/app_configs_landing/  exists and
      contains three files whose contents are byte-identical to the originals
      listed above.  
    • No additional files are present in that destination directory.  
    • Directory /home/user/sync_reports/  exists and contains the file
      latest_sync.log  with this exact content (5 lines, \n line endings):

        SYNC REPORT
        SOURCE: /home/user/app_configs/
        DESTINATION: /home/user/remote_host/app_configs_landing/
        FILES_COPIED: 3
        STATUS: SUCCESS

      The file must be world-readable (mode 644 or more permissive).
name: task_000000_9f55f3c2
